title: This secret San Francisco park seriously feels like you're in a ...
content: the views are absolutely epic. Surrounded by trees, stone pathways, and lake views, it feels surprisingly far from the busy streets

title: Golden Gate Park, San Francisco, California, United States
content: Golden Gate Park is hands down one of the best urban parks in the country, with a mix of leisure, historic, and cultural adventures suited for visitors of any age. Across its acres, you could easily stumble upon a drum circle or yoga class, catch an exhibit at a world-class San Francisco museum, sit in a serene garden with a good book, and walk or bike for miles, all in a day. The Conservatory of Flowers, the oldest building in the park, is a Victorian greenhouse home to a diverse and worldly collection of plants and blooms. A day spent in Golden Gate Park is a good one. $$$ | United States, San Francisco, 757 Market St. As stone's throw from San Francisco's cultural institutions, the Four Seasons combines modern luxury with a great location. $$$ | United States, San Francisco, 125 3rd St. The St. Regis is a proper luxury hotel, with amenities worth checking in for and prime SoMA location.

title: Sweetest park in SF to just relax? - San Francisco Forum - Tripadvisor
content: I know there are a few famous parks in the city. If I just want to spend a few hours lazily walking around or laying in the grass admirering the scenery where would you go? San Francisco probably has as much open space in proportion to the size of the City as any other urban area in the U.S. The Marina is a good area to just hang out, and you can see the Golden Gate Bridge, boats on the water, and the luxury homes across the street. If you want views of the ocean, Sutro Heights and Land's End, by the Cliff House, are good. Sutro Heights was once the estate of a 19th century mayor; the buildings are long gone but the land is now part of the National Park Service and some of Mayor Sutro's statuary and foundations can still be seen. Dolores Park all the way- not only is a great place to relax, it's where all the locals go to sunbathe, hang out, and have an amazing view of the San Francisco skyline!

title: Golden Gate Park guide, moving to San Francisco

title: PANORAMA PARK - San Francisco, California
content: This park has the most amazing views of the bay and the Bay Bridge as well as San Francisco. Make sure you make your way to the top to the Point of Infinity
